name: Azure Firewall Rule Collection Modified or Deleted
conditions_text: when the event(s) were detected by one or more of Microsoft Azure Platform

when the event matches Operation Name (custom) is any of [MICROSOFT.NETWORK/AZUREFIREWALLS/APPLICATIONRULECOLLECTIONS/WRITE or MICROSOFT.NETWORK/AZUREFIREWALLS/APPLICATIONRULECOLLECTIONS/DELETE or MICROSOFT.NETWORK/AZUREFIREWALLS/NATRULECOLLECTIONS/WRITE or MICROSOFT.NETWORK/AZUREFIREWALLS/NATRULECOLLECTIONS/DELETE or MICROSOFT.NETWORK/AZUREFIREWALLS/NETWORKRULECOLLECTIONS/WRITE or MICROSOFT.NETWORK/AZUREFIREWALLS/NETWORKRULECOLLECTIONS/DELETE]
TD_list: APPLY Azure Firewall Rule Collection Modified or Deleted on events which are detected by the LOCAL system
AND when the event(s) were detected by one or more of Microsoft Azure Platform

AND when the event matches Operation Name (custom) is any of [MICROSOFT.NETWORK/AZUREFIREWALLS/APPLICATIONRULECOLLECTIONS/WRITE or MICROSOFT.NETWORK/AZUREFIREWALLS/APPLICATIONRULECOLLECTIONS/DELETE or MICROSOFT.NETWORK/AZUREFIREWALLS/NATRULECOLLECTIONS/WRITE or MICROSOFT.NETWORK/AZUREFIREWALLS/NATRULECOLLECTIONS/DELETE or MICROSOFT.NETWORK/AZUREFIREWALLS/NETWORKRULECOLLECTIONS/WRITE or MICROSOFT.NETWORK/AZUREFIREWALLS/NETWORKRULECOLLECTIONS/DELETE]
RED_list: Event name: Azure Firewall Rule Configuration Modified or Deleted

Event description: Identifies when Rule Collections (Application, NAT, and Network) is being modified or deleted.

falsepositives:
    - Rule Collections (Application, NAT, and Network) being modified or deleted may be performed by a system administrator. Verify whether the user identity, user agent, and/or hostname should be making changes in your environment.
    - Rule Collections (Application, NAT, and Network) modified or deleted from unfamiliar users should be investigated. If known behavior is causing false positives, it can be exempted from the rule.
Force the dispatched event to create a NEW offense, select the offense using Username
Include detected events by "Username" from this point forward, in the offense, for 300 second(s)
This information should contribute to the name of the associated offense(s)
